Sodium tungsten bronzes NaxWO3 with x close to certain values form specific structures. For NaW9O27 (i.e., Na_{1/9}WO3), this corresponds to a very low sodium content tungsten bronze.

At low x values, sodium tungsten bronzes adopt tetragonal structures. The tetragonal tungsten bronze (TTB) structure is common for low-x sodium tungsten bronzes. However, with exactly 37 atoms in the conventional cell and the formula NaW9O27, let me reconsider.

Actually, NaW9O27 with 37 atoms suggests a relatively simple unit cell. Let me think about this as a perovskite-derived structure. WO3 itself has a ReO3-type structure. With Na doping at low levels, a superstructure forms.

A 3×3×1 superstructure of the cubic ReO3-type WO3 would give 9 WO3 units with one Na inserted, forming a tetragonal cell. The cubic WO3 (ReO3-type) has a ≈ 3.78 Å, so a 3×3×1 supercell would be ~11.34 × 11.34 × 3.78 Å. But that gives 9W + 27O + 1Na = 37 atoms in a tetragonal cell with P4/mmm or lower symmetry.
